ddls:
CREATE TABLE "individuals" (
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each individual */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the individual */,
  "workclass" TEXT /* Workclass of the individual (e.g., Private, Self-emp-not-inc) */,
  "fnlwgt" INTEGER /* Final weight of the individual */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the individual (e.g., HS-grad, Masters) */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Education number of the individual */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the individual (e.g., Married-civ-spouse, Never-married) */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the individual (e.g., Exec-managerial, Prof-specialty) */,
  "relationship" TEXT /* Relationship of the individual (e.g., Husband, Not-in-family) */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race of the individual (e.g., White, Black, Asian-Pac-Islander) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the individual (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "capital_gain" INTEGER /* Capital gain of the individual */,
  "capital_loss" INTEGER /* Capital loss of the individual */,
  "hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Hours worked per week by the individual */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of residence */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary of the individual (>50K or <=50K) */,
  "geographic_region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the geographic region */,
  "income_bracket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the income bracket */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the individual */,
  "is_employed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the individual is employed */,
  "employment_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of employment */,
  "employment_end_date" TEXT /* End date of employment */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Current employment status */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update timestamp of the record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_education FOREIGN KEY ("education") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_level"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_race FOREIGN KEY ("race") REFERENCES races ("race"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_relationship FOREIGN KEY ("relationship") REFERENCES relationships ("relationship"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_income_bracket_id FOREIGN KEY ("income_bracket_id") REFERENCES income_brackets ("income_bracket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_country FOREIGN KEY ("country") REFERENCES countries ("country"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_occupation FOREIGN KEY ("occupation") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_workclass FOREIGN KEY ("workclass") REFERENCES workclasses ("workclass"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_geographic_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("geographic_region_id") REFERENCES geographic_regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_salary FOREIGN KEY ("salary") REFERENCES salaries ("salary")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level (e.g., HS-grad, Masters) */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Education number corresponding to the education level */,
  "degree_type" TEXT /* Type of degree (e.g., Bachelor's, Master's, Doctorate) */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study */,
  "is_highest_degree" INTEGER /* Indicates if this is the highest degree obtained by the individual */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workclasses" (
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workclass */,
  "workclass" TEXT /* Workclass (e.g., Private, Self-emp-not-inc) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the workclass */,
  "is_private_sector" INTEGER /* Indicates if the workclass is in the private sector */,
  "is_public_sector" INTEGER /* Indicates if the workclass is in the public sector */,
  "is_self_employed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the individual is self-employed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workclass_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation (e.g., Exec-managerial, Prof-specialty) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the occupation */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the occupation */,
  "is_professional" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is professional */,
  "is_service" INTEGER /* Indicates if the occupation is in the service sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "relationship" TEXT /* Relationship (e.g., Husband, Not-in-family) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the relationship */,
  "is_family_member" INTEGER /* Indicates if the relationship is a family member */,
  "is_spouse" INTEGER /* Indicates if the relationship is a spouse */,
  "is_dependent" INTEGER /* Indicates if the relationship is a dependent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "race" TEXT /* Race (e.g., White, Black, Asian-Pac-Islander) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the race */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity (e.g., Hispanic, Non-Hispanic) */,
  "is_minority" INTEGER /* Indicates if the race is considered a minority */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country (e.g., United-States, India) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region (e.g., North America, Asia) */,
  "subregion" TEXT /* Subregion (e.g., Western Europe, Southern Asia) */,
  "is_developed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the country is developed */,
  "is_developing" INTEGER /* Indicates if the country is developing */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary */,
  "salary" TEXT /* Salary (>50K or <=50K) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the salary */,
  "annual_income" INTEGER /* Annual income */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the salary */,
  "is_taxed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the salary is taxed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "geographic_regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "income_brackets" (
  "income_bracket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income bracket */,
  "bracket_name" TEXT /* Name of the income bracket (e.g., Low, Middle, High) */,
  "min_income" INTEGER /* Minimum income for the bracket */,
  "max_income" INTEGER /* Maximum income for the bracket */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_bracket_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., Census, Survey) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., Government, Private) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_mappings" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the individuals table */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the data_sources table */,
  "data_version" TEXT /* Version of the data */,
  "data_date" TEXT /* Date of the data collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_mappings_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_source_mappings_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id")
)
